quick_facts.label: What this node covers
quick_facts.value: State Police Sub-Inspector recruitment is not a single all-India examination. It is a family of state-specific direct-recruitment processes run through the relevant state police recruitment board; for example, West Bengal Police Recruitment Board (WBPRB) and Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment & Promotion Board (UPPRPB) publish separate SI recruitment notices and results.

quick_facts.label: Typical educational threshold
quick_facts.value: A bachelor’s degree is a common eligibility benchmark, but candidates must rely on the notice for their state and cycle. WBPRB’s 2024 SI recruitment required a bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognised university or equivalent.

quick_facts.label: Selection is multi-stage
quick_facts.value: Written examination alone is generally not the complete selection process. In the WBPRB 2024 SI cycle, the route was preliminary examination, physical measurement test (PMT), physical efficiency test (PET), final combined competitive examination, and personality test, followed by character verification and medical examination for provisionally selected candidates.

quick_facts.label: State-specific conditions matter
quick_facts.value: Age limits, category relaxations, local-language requirements, physical standards, vacancies, fees, and the sequence of tests can differ by state and notification. For example, the WBPRB 2024 notice set an age band of 20–30 years as of its stated cut-off date and required Bengali language ability subject to a hill-area exception.

quick_facts.label: Current-cycle context
quick_facts.value: As of the review date, official boards should be checked directly rather than relying on old calendars: UPPRPB’s notice board records publication of the final selection result for its SI Civil Police and equivalent direct recruitment 2025 on 14 July 2026, while WBPRB’s SI 2024 recruitment page lists its final combined competitive-examination notices from February 2026.

sections.heading: Purpose and scope
paragraphs: This node represents the competitive recruitment route into the rank of Sub-Inspector in a state police force. There is no single national “State Police SI Exam”: recruitment is announced, administered, and scheduled separately by state-level police recruitment authorities, and a drive may cover more than one SI stream or equivalent post. WBPRB, for instance, ran a combined process for Sub-Inspector posts in its Unarmed and Armed Branches; UPPRPB separately publishes notices for direct recruitment to Sub-Inspector Civil Police and equivalent posts.
bullets: Treat the specific state board’s notification as the controlling document for eligibility, vacancies, exam scheme, reservation, physical criteria, fees, and dates.
Do not assume that a notification from one state establishes a rule for another state.

sections.heading: Eligibility: verify the notification for the target state
paragraphs: A bachelor’s degree is a frequent entry requirement for direct SI recruitment, but the exact qualification date and recognition rules are notification-specific. In WBPRB’s 2024 process, applicants needed a bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognised university or equivalent, and had to possess the stated qualifications by the online-application deadline.

Age, citizenship, language, reservation documentation, and physical standards must likewise be checked against the live notification. As a dated example only, WBPRB’s 2024 notice required Indian citizenship; an age of 20–30 years on 1 January 2024 with stated relaxations; and Bengali speaking, reading, and writing ability, subject to the published exception for permanent residents of the Darjeeling and Kalimpong hill subdivisions.
bullets: Confirm the cut-off date for degree completion and age before applying.
Check whether the state requires a local-language qualification or provides a specified exemption.
Read physical-measurement tables carefully: standards can vary by branch, gender/category, and state.
Keep category, EWS, domicile, identity, educational, and—where applicable—employer NOC documents ready in the form and validity period stipulated by the recruiting board.

sections.heading: Exam and selection format
paragraphs: The format varies by state. A concrete official example is WBPRB’s 2024 SI process: a 200-mark preliminary OMR/MCQ screening examination in General Studies, logical and analytical reasoning, and arithmetic; PMT and PET for candidates clearing the screening stage; a 200-mark final written examination with three conventional-question papers; and a 30-mark personality test. WBPRB stated that preliminary marks were not counted toward final merit and that incorrect preliminary answers attracted one-fourth negative marking.

Physical testing is a qualifying gateway, not merely an administrative formality. In that WBPRB cycle, the notice prescribed PMT standards and a timed run for PET; successful candidates then progressed to the final written stage. The board’s recruitment page also documents the actual sequence of preliminary result, PMT/PET, and final examination notices for that cycle.
bullets: Build preparation around the notification’s stated scheme, not a generic coaching template.
Expect written aptitude/general-studies testing plus physical measurement and efficiency assessments in many state SI recruitments.
Check whether the final written stage is objective, descriptive/conventional, or mixed; language papers and interview/personality-test stages are state-dependent.

sections.heading: Application process
paragraphs: Apply only through the portal and within the window named in the relevant state notification. In the WBPRB 2024 example, applications were online only; candidates chose their preferred SI branch while applying, uploaded a recent photograph and signature, and paid the notification-specific fee through approved online payment methods. That cycle’s application period and fee amounts are historical examples, not current universal deadlines or charges.

Before final submission, verify every field and save the application record and payment proof. WBPRB’s notice warned that later corrections could be restricted and that candidates advancing to later stages had to produce originals and supporting reservation/qualification documents. State boards also issue later notices for admit cards, answer keys, physical-test calls, results, and final selection, so applicants should monitor the official recruitment page after applying.
bullets: Register through the official state recruitment-board portal linked in the notification.
Upload documents and photographs exactly to the prescribed specifications; retain the submitted application and transaction record.
Download admit cards only from official links, and check official notices for changes to venue, schedule, document verification, or physical-test instructions.
Treat application dates and fees as cycle-specific; confirm them from the current notification before payment.

sections.heading: Preparation strategy
paragraphs: Start with the syllabus and marking rules from the target state’s current notification. WBPRB’s published 2024 scheme illustrates the breadth candidates may need to cover: General Studies/general knowledge, logical and analytical reasoning, arithmetic at the specified school-level standard, English, and a selected regional-language paper at the final stage. Its preliminary stage was time-limited and used negative marking, so timed accuracy practice was important for that particular cycle.

Prepare physical fitness in parallel with written study. The WBPRB example used a qualifying run test after physical measurement, and its stated minimum run distances/times differed by candidate category. Candidates should train only for the standards in their own notice and schedule recovery, injury prevention, and mock test work well before the physical-test call letter.
bullets: Map every topic, paper, duration, and negative-marking rule from the official syllabus into a weekly plan.
Use timed practice for arithmetic, reasoning, and general-studies questions; review errors rather than only increasing test volume.
Practise report writing, translation, grammar, and précis if the notification contains descriptive English or regional-language papers.
Measure baseline running capacity early and train progressively toward the notification’s PET requirement; also check PMT standards before committing to the process.

sections.heading: Outcome: appointment as a State Police Sub-Inspector
paragraphs: Candidates who clear all prescribed stages and place high enough in the state-specific merit process may be provisionally selected for appointment as a Sub-Inspector in the advertised branch or stream. In WBPRB’s 2024 model, final merit was based on final written-examination marks plus personality-test marks; candidates also had to meet the personality-test minimum, after which character-antecedent verification and medical examination applied to provisionally selected candidates.

Selection does not mean that every procedural requirement has ended. The WBPRB notice states that the appointing authority depends on the branch/post and that medical fitness, document verification, and character verification remain material conditions. The exact service terms, pay level, probation, training, posting, and branch allocation must be taken from the state’s applicable rules and appointment communication rather than inferred from another state’s recruitment.
bullets: The direct career outcome is entry into the state police force as a Sub-Inspector, subject to the advertised branch and appointment process.
Final results, vacancies, and appointment timing are variable; follow the official board’s result notices and subsequent department instructions.
